required_relationships: Represent ray/vector cues as thin glowing curve_polyline or dotted tracers, not thick arrow primitives.
Keep distinct vector/ray cues spatially separated enough to read individually.
incident_white_ray enters from the left toward glass_prism
glass_prism stays centered on dark_matte_optical_bench
surface_normal_entry is perpendicular to the left prism face at the entry point
surface_normal_exit is perpendicular to the right prism face at the exit point
spectrum_red_ray bends least, spectrum_green_ray stays between, and spectrum_blue_ray bends most
projection_screen stands to the right of glass_prism
red_screen_dot, green_screen_dot, and blue_screen_dot sit separated on projection_screen at the ray endpoints
forbidden_failures: Do not use thick arrows, arrow primitives, visible light bars, or foreground light panels.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: glass_prism.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: incident_white_ray.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: surface_normal_entry.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: surface_normal_exit.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: internal_ray.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: spectrum_red_ray.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: spectrum_green_ray.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: spectrum_blue_ray.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: projection_screen.
Do not abstract away scene anchor: red_screen_dot.
glass_prism replaced by a flat opaque triangle
projection_screen replaced by a blank glowing rectangle with no RGB dots
RGB rays collapsed into one white or rainbow smear
surface normals shown as generic axes unrelated to prism faces
formula blocks floating over the center of the prism
optical bench removed into a pure black void
decorative abstract particles substituted for the actual ray geometry
